zh.lifeSummary: Matilda de Estur是* Baldwin de ESTUR的女兒，出生在英國哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特。她於大約1239年與Walter de Insula在英國英格蘭哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特伍頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1280年在英國英格蘭哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Estur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: perhaps a topographic name from Middle High German ezzisch ‘seed field’ + tor ‘gate’.

variants.name: Esther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe.
Jewish (from Syria): perhaps a nickname from a vernacular form of standard Arabic ‘uṣaṣ ‘coccyx’.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metronymic from the Yiddish personal name Ese, a pet form of Ester (see Esther ), + Yiddish possessive -s.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Matilda de Estur was born in Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England as the daughter of * Baldwin de ESTUR. She married Walter de Insula about 1239, in Wootton, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1280, in Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de Estur(은)는 영국 함프실르 이슬일 프 와이트에서 * Baldwin de ESTUR의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1239년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 이슬일 프 와이트 보턴에서 Walter de Insula(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1280년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 이슬일 프 와이트에서 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Walter de Insula aproximadamente 1239, em Wootton, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Matilda de Estur родилась в местности исл оф ваит, хампшир, Англия. Её отца звали * Baldwin de ESTUR. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter de Insula около 1239 г. в местности вуттон, исл оф ваит, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1280 г. в местности исл оф ваит, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания.
